short.en: A 3D-printed whistle tuned to musical A — designed from acoustic first principles.
short.sl: 3D natisnjena piščalka, uglašena na ton A — zasnovana iz akustičnih prvih načel.

description.en: A Helmholtz-resonator whistle whose cavity dimensions were calculated, not guessed. Target frequency 440 Hz (concert A), printed in PETG, verified with a tuner. The case study walks through the resonator equation, the volume math, and the tweak that brought it back into tune after a first failed print.
description.sl: Piščalka tipa Helmholtz, katere mere votline so bile izračunane, ne uganjene. Ciljna frekvenca 440 Hz (koncertni A), tiskana iz PETG, preverjena z uglaševalnikom. Študija primera razloži enačbo resonatorja, izračun prostornine in popravek, ki jo je po prvem neuspešnem tisku vrnil v točno glasbo.

details.en: Helmholtz frequency f = (c / 2π) · √(A / (V · L)), with c = 343 m/s. Solving for V at f = 440 Hz with neck area A = 12 mm² and effective neck length L = 8 mm gives V ≈ 11.5 cm³. The resonant cavity was modeled as a cylinder of Ø 28 mm × 19 mm height to match. The first print landed at 426 Hz; trimming the neck length by 0.6 mm corrected the pitch.
details.sl: Frekvenca po Helmholtzu f = (c / 2π) · √(A / (V · L)), z c = 343 m/s. Z f = 440 Hz, presekom vratu A = 12 mm² in efektivno dolžino vratu L = 8 mm dobimo V ≈ 11,5 cm³. Resonančna votlina je modelirana kot valj Ø 28 mm × 19 mm. Prvi tisk je pristal pri 426 Hz; skrajšanje vratu za 0,6 mm je popravilo višino tona.
